name: Lemon-Herb Orzo
description: The Mediterranean Dish's toasted orzo, kept as a bright lemon-herb version (sun-dried tomatoes and parmesan held back, since it's serving under the tomato-feta Greek shrimp). Orzo toasted in olive oil for a nutty edge, then tossed with garlic, lemon, parsley, and dill. 15 minutes.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup plus 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
(held back for this pairing: 1/3 cup sun-dried tomatoes; 1/2–3/4 cup grated parmesan; optional red pepper flakes)
recipeInstructions: Toast the orzo in a large saucepan with 2 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high until golden brown.
Add 7 cups boiling water, season with salt, and cook to al dente (7-8 minutes). Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain.
Warm 1/2 cup olive oil in a large pan over medium. Add the minced garlic and a pinch of salt; cook until fragrant.
Add 1/2 cup reserved pasta water and the lemon juice; bring to a boil. Stir in the parsley and dill.

Add the drained orzo and toss to combine; season with salt and pepper. Add more pasta water if it looks dry. (Skip the sun-dried tomatoes and parmesan so it stays a clean lemon-herb orzo under the shrimp.)
author: The Mediterranean Dish

ai.cliffNotes: Toast orzo in olive oil, boil to al dente, then toss with a garlic-lemon-herb oil. Kept plain (no sun-dried tomato/parmesan) so it's a clean bed for the tomato-feta shrimp. 15 min.
keyTips: Toasting the orzo in good olive oil before boiling brings out a nutty depth — don't skip it.
Reserve a cup of the starchy pasta water and use a splash to loosen the orzo at the end; it gives a glossy, just-saucy texture without more oil.
For this pairing, hold the sun-dried tomatoes and parmesan from the original recipe — the Greek shrimp is already rich with tomato and feta, so a clean lemon-herb orzo lets it shine.
Cook the orzo just to al dente; it keeps absorbing as it sits, so a slightly firm orzo ends up perfect on the plate.
ai.source: Claude (prose-mined from The Mediterranean Dish article)
